text.title: ruby-sidekiq-jobs Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for ruby-sidekiq-jobs. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every job must be idempotent: check record.processed? or equivalent at the start of perform and return early if true.
rule.rationale: Sidekiq delivers at-least-once; network partitions and worker crashes trigger retries. Without a guard, a payment job may charge twice or an email may send twice.

rule.statement: Pass record IDs not ActiveRecord objects to perform; re-fetch inside perform with Model.find(id).
rule.rationale: Sidekiq 7 strict args mode raises on non-JSON-serializable objects. Even when tolerated, the record may be stale or deleted by the time the job runs. ID re-fetch is always current.

rule.statement: Enable Sidekiq.strict_args!(true) in the initializer to catch non-serializable arguments at enqueue time.
rule.rationale: Without strict mode, passing an ActiveRecord object serializes silently in legacy versions and raises cryptically in newer ones. Fail fast at enqueue rather than at dequeue.

rule.statement: Set sidekiq_options retry: N, dead: true explicitly on every job class; never rely on the default of 25 retries.
rule.rationale: 25 retries over 21 days is rarely correct for user-facing jobs. Explicit retry: 3 or retry: 5 matches real SLA expectations and keeps the dead set from filling with stale jobs.

rule.statement: Define sidekiq_retry_in per exception class: linear backoff for rate-limit errors, exponential for transient failures.
rule.rationale: A payment gateway rate-limiting error needs shorter, linear retries to clear the rate limit window; a generic network error benefits from exponential backoff to avoid hammering a recovering service.

rule.statement: Rescue only ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound (log and return) and known transient errors (re-raise). Let all other exceptions propagate to trigger Sidekiq retry.
rule.rationale: Swallowing StandardError defeats the retry mechanism. Sidekiq retries on exception; a rescued, swallowed error looks like a successful job.

rule.statement: Keep job classes thin: perform calls a service object that holds the logic and is unit-testable without Sidekiq.
rule.rationale: Service objects can be tested with plain RSpec + Mockery without Sidekiq::Testing infrastructure, making unit tests faster and more isolated.
